event: Cappella Ruccelai (Shrine of the Holy Sepulchre)
symbols: Latin inscription 'YHESUM QUERITIS' — 'You seek Jesus,' the angel’s words at the empty tomb
sepulchre form — a miniature replica of Christ’s burial place in Jerusalem
decorative medallions — sacred geometry reflecting divine order
crosses atop the structure — the centrality of Christ’s death and resurrection
description: A photograph of a small marble architectural structure inside a church. The structure resembles a miniature classical building with pilasters, Corinthian capitals, and an entablature. A Latin inscription 'YHESUM QUERITIS N' runs across the upper portion. Below, marble panels feature circular decorative medallions with floral and geometric patterns in colored stone inlay. A small barred opening or gate is visible in one panel. The top is crowned with Gothic-style crosses or finials. An additional Latin inscription appears on the lower portion.
theological_description: This chapel, designed by Leon Battista Alberti, is a miniature replica of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem—the tomb where Christ’s body was laid after the crucifixion (Matthew 27:59-60) and from which he rose on the third day. The prominent Latin inscription 'YHESUM QUERITIS' (‘You seek Jesus’) echoes the angel’s words to the women at the empty tomb: ‘You seek Jesus who was crucified. He is not here, for he has risen’ (Matthew 28:5-6). This inscription transforms the architectural monument into a proclamation of the resurrection. The structure serves as a material witness to the central event of Christian faith: ‘If Christ has not been raised, your faith is in vain’ (1 Corinthians 15:17). By replicating the Holy Sepulchre, the chapel brings the reality of pilgrimage to Jerusalem into the local worshiping community.

scripture_refs.reason: The angel’s words at the empty tomb—‘You seek Jesus’—inscribed on the structure
Joseph of Arimathea placing Jesus’ body in the new tomb, which this structure replicates
If Christ has not been raised, faith is in vain—the empty tomb as proof of resurrection
He has risen, he is not here. See the place where they laid him
